verses.commentary: After Jesus said this, He looked toward heaven and prayed: Father, the hour has come. Glorify Your Son, that Your Son may glorify You. High Priestly Prayer begins. Eyes heavenward. The hour arrives. Mutual glorification requested. Son's glory enables Father's glory.
Purpose: give eternal life. To given ones. Divine sovereignty in salvation. Election evident.
Ginōskōsin (γινώσκωσιν), know. Relational knowledge. True God. Jesus Christ sent. Knowing God and Jesus is life itself.
Edoxasa (ἐδόξασα), aorist, I glorified. Telesios (τελειώσας), finishing. Cross imminent but work counted as done. Anticipatory completion.
Pre-incarnate glory. Before creation. Return to original state. Exaltation prayer.

I have revealed You to those whom You gave Me out of the world. They were Yours; You gave them to Me and they have obeyed Your word. Revelation accomplished. Given ones identified. Ek tou kosmou (ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου), out of the world. Father's possession transferred to Son. Obedience marks them.
All Jesus has is from Father. Source acknowledged.
Received. Knowledge and belief. Certainty of origin. Faith in mission.
Not for world. For given ones. They belong to Father. Particularity of prayer.
Everything shared between Father and Son. Disciples glorify Jesus. Their faith brings Him honor.
They stay in hostile world. Prayer for protection. Divine name as guardian. Unity requested. Modeled on Trinitarian unity.
Jesus guarded them. None lost. Except Judas. Son of perdition. Apolleia (ἀπώλεια), destruction. Psalm 41:9, 109:8 fulfilled.

I am coming to You now, but I say these things while I am still in the world, so that they may have the full measure of My joy within them. Joy purpose. Prayer spoken for their benefit. Full joy. Peplērōmenēn (πεπληρωμένην), completed. Jesus' joy in them.
Hatred follows. Non-belonging to world. Like Jesus, set apart. Otherness produces hostility.
They remain in world. Guarded from Satan. Ek tou ponērou (ἐκ τοῦ πονηροῦ), from the evil one. Preservation, not extraction.
Non-belonging. Jesus and disciples share alien status. In world, not of it.
Truth as agent. Word is truth. Progressive holiness through Scripture.
Jesus sent by Father. Disciples sent by Jesus. Apostolic commissioning. Into hostile world.
For their sake. His setting apart enables theirs. Sacrificial sanctification. Cross sanctifies.

My prayer is not for them alone. I pray also for those who will believe in Me through their message. Extended scope. Future believers included. Through apostolic word. All subsequent Christians. Prayer spans centuries.
Model: Trinitarian oneness. Believers in Father and Son. Purpose: world's belief. Unity is evangelistic.
I have given them the glory that You gave Me, that they may be one as We are one. Glory given. Shared divine glory. For unity. Oneness through glory.

I in them and You in Me, so that they may be brought to complete unity. Then the world will know that You sent Me and have loved them even as You have loved me. Indwelling. Teteleiōmenoi (τετελειωμένοι), perfected, completed. Perfect unity. World's knowledge goal. Jesus' mission validated. Father's love for believers equals love for Son. Astounding claim.

Father, I want those You have given Me to be with Me where I am, and to see My glory, the glory You have given Me because You loved Me before the creation of the world. Desire expressed. Thelō (θέλω), I will, desire. Presence request. See His glory. Pre-creation love. Eternal relationship.

Righteous Father, though the world does not know You, I know You, and they know that You have sent Me. Dikaie Pater (δίκαιε πάτερ), righteous Father. World's ignorance. Jesus' knowledge. Disciples' knowledge of mission. Contrasting knowledge.

I have made You known to them, and will continue to make You known in order that the love You have for Me may be in them and that I Myself may be in them. Revelation mission. Past and future. Purpose: Father's love in disciples. Jesus in disciples. Love and presence imparted.
